SUBJECTIVE:
Milo, 4y MN Labrador; Diarrhoea since yesterday morning, first noted about 6am yesterday, 6-7 episodes yesterday and 3 this morning; Stool watery with mucus, tiny red streak once last night; One vomit yesterday afternoon, yellow/foamy, none overnight; Appetite lower, drinking slightly more, slightly quieter but still waggy/wanting to go out, urgency overnight with no house accident; Possible dietary indiscretion at park (sausage/bread), daycare contact with dog with loose stools, Pro-Kolin approx 2 ml BID yesterday, midday dosing may be difficult.

OBJECTIVE:
Bright, responsive, friendly, slightly quieter than typical; wt 28.4 kg; T 38.7C, HR 108, RR 28; MM pink/moist; CRT<2; Mild cranial abdo discomfort on deep palpation, no obvious mass, no severe guarding; Lungs clear, heart sounds normal rhythm, pulses good quality; Mild dehydration approx 5%.

ASSESSMENT:
Acute gastroenteritis, likely dietary indiscretion; Stable for outpatient management today; no current indication for immediate hospitalisation.

PLAN:
Maropitant 1 mg/kg SC in clinic; Pro-Kolin 5 ml PO q8h x3 days; bland diet small frequent meals x3 days, then transition to normal food over 2-3 days if stools improve; Encourage water; avoid high-fat treats and no new foods this week; short lead walks only today; Monitor for repeated vomiting, unable to keep water down, increasing blood in stool, marked lethargy, abdominal pain, or no improvement by 48 hours; Nurse progress call tomorrow at 15:30; if still loose/not improving, recheck and faecal testing; Stool pot and emailed care plan on checkout; reception to tag case for 48h review if no improvement.
